sections.title: Getting started
Talking to Sanfew

lines.speaker: Sanfew
lines.text: What can I do for you young 'un?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have you any more work for me, to help reclaim the circle?

lines.speaker: Sanfew
lines.text: Not just yet, young 'un.

lines.speaker: Sanfew
lines.text: 'm afraid you are not yet learned enough in the art of Herblore to be able to help me, adventurer...

lines.speaker: Sanfew
lines.text: Ah, you've come just in time. I need a certain herb for the next part of the purification ritual...

lines.speaker: Sanfew
lines.text: It used to be quite common, but nowadays only the trolls know where to find it. They use it in their cooking, you see. It's a very prized ingredient.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And what exactly do you want me to do?

lines.speaker: Sanfew
lines.text: Journey to the north into the land of the trolls, and find the secret of the herb the trolls call 'goutweed'.

lines.speaker: Sanfew
lines.text: My friend Eadgar lives in the area, and he may be able to help you. Bring some goutweed back and I will teach you something useful.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll do it.

lines.speaker: Sanfew
lines.text: Thank you, adventurer!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks.

lines.speaker: Sanfew
lines.text: Oh well. No doubt some other adventurer will eventually come who can help me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, I don't need to speak to you.

lines.speaker: Sanfew
lines.text: Well, we all make mistakes sometimes.

sections.title: Talking to Sanfew after starting the quest

lines.speaker: Sanfew
lines.text: What can I do for you young 'un?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What was I meant to be doing again?

lines.speaker: Sanfew
lines.text: I've told you already.

lines.speaker: Sanfew
lines.text: Journey to the north into the land of the trolls, and find the secret of the herb the trolls call goutweed.

lines.speaker: Sanfew
lines.text: My friend Eadgar lives in the area, and he may be able to help you. Bring some goutweed back and I will teach you something useful.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll get on with it.

sections.title: Searching the laundry basket
Talking to Tegid

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, you're doing laundry, eh?

lines.speaker: Tegid
lines.text: Yes. What is it to you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nice day for it.

lines.speaker: Tegid
lines.text: I suppose it is.

sections.title: Searching the rack
Finding Eadgar
Taking to Eadgar before talking to Burntmeat

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Eadgar
lines.text: Welcome to Mad Eadgar's! Happiness in a bowl!

lines.speaker: Eadgar
lines.text: Would you care to sample our delicious home cooking?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to find some goutweed. Sanfew said you might be able to help.

lines.speaker: Eadgar
lines.text: Sanfew, you say? Ah, haven't seen him in a while...

lines.speaker: Eadgar
lines.text: Goutweed is used as an ingredient in troll cooking. You should ask one of their cooks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why do you live so close to the trolls? Isn't it dangerous?

lines.speaker: Eadgar
lines.text: Well, I suppose I do keep getting captured by the trolls and thrown in prison...But they always release me in the end, I'm far too old and skinny for their tastes.

lines.speaker: Eadgar
lines.text: In any case, this is my home, and I'm not leaving it. And this area has the tastiest goats!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you have to offer?

lines.speaker: Eadgar
lines.text: The chef's recommendation for today is mountain goat stew. I'll give some stew in exchange for logs for my fire. They're hard to come by around here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd like some mountain goat stew, please.

lines.speaker: Eadgar
lines.text: Here you go! Enjoy!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks, Eadgar.

lines.speaker: Eadgar
lines.text: Your loss!

sections.title: Taking to Eadgar again before talking to Burntmeat
Talking to Burntmeat

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er, hi.

lines.speaker: Burntmeat
lines.text: Hmm? What human do in troll kitchen? Burntmeat tired of cooking goats. Human look tasty.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, you don't want to eat me! I'm a tough, hardened adventurer, not tender or tasty at all!

lines.speaker: Burntmeat
lines.text: Hmm. Burntmeat think you probably right. What human doing here?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm on a quest to find some goutweed.

lines.speaker: Burntmeat
lines.text: Bwahahaha! Burntmeat not give his greatest cooking secret away so easily.

lines.speaker: Burntmeat
lines.text: But Burntmeat also has quest for human!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Really? What is it?

lines.speaker: Burntmeat
lines.text: Bring back a tasty human for Burntmeat's stew. If you find tasty human, Burntmeat will give you good reward.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right. I'll just...go fetch that for you then. Bye!

sections.title: Talking to Burntmeat again

lines.speaker: Burntmeat
lines.text: Did you find tasty human?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Erm, not yet, but I'm working on it...

sections.title: Taking to Eadgar after talking to Burntmeat

lines.speaker: Eadgar
lines.text: Oh, it's you. Have you talked to the troll cook yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to find some goutweed. Sanfew said you might be able to help.

lines.speaker: Eadgar
lines.text: Sanfew, you say? Ah, haven't seen him in a while...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I talked to the troll cook but he wouldn't tell me anything, and now he wants me to find him some tasty human for his stew.

lines.speaker: Eadgar
lines.text: Oh dear, that's no good. You can't just go hand over a human to those trolls...

lines.speaker: Eadgar
lines.text: Aha! I have a plan!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Eadgar
lines.text: Yes! It's bound to work. First of all, I will need a parrot!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A PARROT? Where am I going to find one of those?

lines.speaker: Eadgar
lines.text: At the zoo, where else?

lines.speaker: Eadgar
lines.text: Anything else I can do for you?

sections.title: Taking to Eadgar again before getting a parrot

lines.speaker: Eadgar
lines.text: Oh, it's you. Have you got me a parrot yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I haven't been able to find one yet.

lines.speaker: Eadgar
lines.text: Well, go see if the zoo have one.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where's the zoo?

lines.speaker: Eadgar
lines.text: It's in Ardougne, south west of here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll be right back.

lines.speaker: Eadgar
lines.text: Anything else I can do for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll be right back.

lines.speaker: Eadgar
lines.text: Anything else I can do for you?

sections.title: Visiting Ardougne
Using pineapple chunks on vodka before talking to Parroty Pete
Talking to Parroty Pete

lines.speaker: Parroty Pete
lines.text: Good day, good day. Come to admire the new parrot aviary have we?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's very nice.

lines.speaker: Parroty Pete
lines.text: Isn't it just?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: When did you add it?

lines.speaker: Parroty Pete
lines.text: Just recently. It would have been sooner, but some wretch thought it would be amusing to replace their drinking water with vodka. The vet had to nurse them back to health for weeks!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you feed them?

lines.speaker: Parroty Pete
lines.text: Well, fruit and grain mostly. I try to give them a balanced diet, but their favourite treat is pineapple chunks.

sections.title: Using vodka on the aviary hatch

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: They won't drink it straight from the bottle...

sections.title: Using pineapple chunks on the aviary hatch

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need some way to slow it down...

sections.title: Using alco-chunks on the hatch

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hah! Got you now!

lines.speaker: Parrot
lines.text: Sqwaawk...*hic*

lines.speaker: Parroty Pete
lines.text: Hey! What are you doing with that parrot?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nothing.

lines.speaker: Parroty Pete
lines.text: It looks drunk! You should NEVER feed alcohol to a parrot!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, good thing I found it! I'll just take it to the vet for you, shall I?

lines.speaker: Parroty Pete
lines.text: Oh, thank you! We're ever so busy here at the Zoo.

sections.title: Preparing the fake man
Talking to Eadgar while holding the parrot

lines.speaker: Eadgar
lines.text: Oh, it's you. Have you got me a parrot yet?

lines.speaker: Parrot
lines.text: Raaawk! Polly wanna cracker!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here it is! Now are you going to explain your plan?

lines.speaker: Eadgar
lines.text: Yes, yes, of course. It's quite ingenious really!

lines.speaker: Eadgar
lines.text: What we need is something that looks like a human, sounds like a human, smells like a human and tastes like a human.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How are we going to make it look like a human?

lines.speaker: Eadgar
lines.text: We'll just make a scarecrow. We'll need logs, and 10 sheaves of wheat for stuffing.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How are we going to make it sound like a human?

lines.speaker: Eadgar
lines.text: That's what the parrot's for, of course.

lines.speaker: Parrot
lines.text: Who's a pretty boy then?

lines.speaker: Eadgar
lines.text: Although the trolls might get suspicious if it doesn't say what they expect a human to say...

lines.speaker: Eadgar
lines.text: You should hide it for a while somewhere it can pick up some more appropriate catchphrases.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How are we going to make it smell like a human?

lines.speaker: Eadgar
lines.text: Well, we'll need to dress it up anyway; if we use dirty clothes it'll smell human to the trolls.

lines.speaker: Eadgar
lines.text: I'm a man of few clothes myself, so you might want to ask old Sanfew about that one.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And how are we going to make it taste like a human?

lines.speaker: Eadgar
lines.text: That's easy, we'll just stuff it with a few chickens. Everything tastes like chicken! Five raw chickens should be enough.

lines.speaker: Eadgar
lines.text: That's the plan. Anything else I can do for you?

sections.title: Talking to Eadgar before preparing the fake man

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What should I do with this parrot?

lines.speaker: Eadgar
lines.text: I told you, put it somewhere it'll learn to say the sort of thing the trolls will expect it to say.

lines.speaker: Eadgar
lines.text: This is likely to be screaming. There's bound to be a good spot somewhere in the troll prison.

lines.speaker: Eadgar
lines.text: Anything else I can do for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How are we going to make it look like a human?

lines.speaker: Eadgar
lines.text: We'll just make a scarecrow. We'll need logs, and 10 sheaves of wheat for stuffing.

lines.speaker: Eadgar
lines.text: Anything else I can do for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How are we going to make it sound like a human?

lines.speaker: Eadgar
lines.text: That's what the parrot's for, of course.

lines.speaker: Eadgar
lines.text: You should hide it for a while somewhere it can pick up some more appropriate catchphrases.

lines.speaker: Eadgar
lines.text: Anything else I can do for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How are we going to make it smell like a human?

lines.speaker: Eadgar
lines.text: Well, we'll need to dress it up anyway; if we use dirty clothes it'll smell human to the trolls.

lines.speaker: Eadgar
lines.text: I'm a man of few clothes myself, so you might want to ask old Sanfew about that one.

lines.speaker: Eadgar
lines.text: Anything else I can do for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And how are we going to make it taste like a human?

lines.speaker: Eadgar
lines.text: That's easy, we'll just stuff it with a few chickens. Everything tastes like chicken! Five raw chickens should be enough.

lines.speaker: Eadgar
lines.text: Anything else I can do for you?

sections.title: Using drunk parrot on rack
Search rack after putting parrot under it

lines.speaker: Parrot
lines.text: Polly wanna cracker!

lines.speaker: Parrot
lines.text: Pieces of eight! Pieces of eight!

lines.speaker: Parrot
lines.text: Who's a pretty boy then?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't think it's done yet.

sections.title: Talking to Eadgar after putting the parrot under the rack

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I hid the parrot under the rack in the troll prison.

lines.speaker: Eadgar
lines.text: Good work. How about those other items?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have [no/the] logs, [no/one/two/three/four/five] [chicken/chickens], [no/one/two/three/four/five/six/seven/eight/nine/ten] [bundle/bundles] of grain and [no/the] dirty clothes.

lines.speaker: Eadgar
lines.text: You now need [no/the] logs, [no/one/two/three/four/five] [chicken/chickens], [no/one/two/three/four/five/six/seven/eight/nine/ten] [bundle/bundles] of grain and [no/the] dirty clothes.

lines.speaker: Eadgar
lines.text: Anything else I can do for you?

sections.title: Obtaining the robes
Talking to Sanfew about the robes

lines.speaker: Sanfew
lines.text: What can I do for you young 'un?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Eadgar says he needs some dirty clothes for his plan, and he said you might be able to help.

lines.speaker: Sanfew
lines.text: Now why would he need that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's a long story.

lines.speaker: Sanfew
lines.text: Never mind then... I think Tegid is doing his laundry outside. You could ask him if you can borrow one of his dirty robes.

sections.title: Talking to Tegid about the robes

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, you're doing laundry, eh?

lines.speaker: Tegid
lines.text: Yes. What is it to you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nice day for it.

lines.speaker: Tegid
lines.text: I suppose it is.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You wouldn't be able to spare any of those dirty robes by any chance? It's a matter of the utmost importance.

lines.speaker: Tegid
lines.text: What? No! These are my robes!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fine.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sure Sanfew won't be happy when I tell him it's your fault he can't perform the purification ritual.

lines.speaker: Tegid
lines.text: What? Oh well, if it's a matter of that much importance, I suppose you can borrow one...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You'll give me those robes right now...or I'm going to cut down trees until you do.

lines.speaker: Tegid
lines.text: You wouldn't dare!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course I would. They're just trees.

lines.speaker: Tegid
lines.text: You monster! Take the robes and leave this place!

sections.title: Returning to Eadgar with supplies
Talking to Eadgar with the supplies

lines.speaker: Eadgar
lines.text: How are you getting on?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have [no/the] logs, [no/one/two/three/four/five] [chicken/chickens], [no/one/two/three/four/five/six/seven/eight/nine/ten] [bundle/bundles] of grain and [no/the] dirty clothes.

lines.speaker: Eadgar
lines.text: That's everything!

lines.speaker: Eadgar
lines.text: Good, good, everything is almost finished.

lines.speaker: Eadgar
lines.text: Of course, we can't just give him the dummy and expect to get anything useful out of him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh?

lines.speaker: Eadgar
lines.text: No, of course not. We need to make sure he'll tell you what you need to know.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And how do we do that?

lines.speaker: Eadgar
lines.text: I happen to know that the trolls are susceptible to a certain kind of plant that grows around this mountain. I call it Troll Thistle. If properly prepared, it can be made into a sort of Troll truth potion.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do you prepare it?

lines.speaker: Eadgar
lines.text: You'll have to dry it in a fire, then grind it and mix it into a potion with Ranarr weed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll be back with that soon.

lines.speaker: Eadgar
lines.text: Anything else I can do for you?

sections.title: Talking to Eadgar after learning to make troll potion

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I make the troll truth potion again?

lines.speaker: Eadgar
lines.text: Troll Thistle grows around this mountain. If properly prepared, it can be made into a sort of Troll truth potion.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do you prepare it?

lines.speaker: Eadgar
lines.text: You'll have to dry it in a fire, then grind it and mix it into a potion with Ranarr weed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll be back with that soon.

lines.speaker: Eadgar
lines.text: Anything else I can do for you?

sections.title: Picking a troll thistle
Using troll thistle on ranarr potion
Using any item on Eadgar's fire

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd better not mess around with Eadgar's stew!

sections.title: Using troll thistle on a fire
Using dried thistle on ranarr potion
Using dried thistle on pestle and mortar
Using ground thistle on ranarr potion
Talking to Eadgar with the troll potion

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've got the troll truth potion.

lines.speaker: Eadgar
lines.text: Excellent, thank you. Now just go fetch that poor parrot back, it's probably had enough by now.

sections.title: Taking the parrot from under the rack

lines.speaker: Parrot
lines.text: Ah, hello Sir. Could you please free me? I seem to have ... OW! What are you doing? That's my spleen!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think it's probably heard enough.

sections.title: Using the educated parrot on the rack
Using alco-chunks on the aviary hatch after losing the educated parrot

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The parrott flew off, I wonder where it went? Silly thing prefered Eadgar to me!

sections.title: Talking to Eadgar after taking the parrot out from the rack

lines.speaker: Eadgar
lines.text: Oh, it's you. You didn't lose your parrot did you? One flew over here and I managed to capture it.

lines.speaker: Eadgar
lines.text: That's what I thought.

lines.speaker: Eadgar
lines.text: Just give me a moment...there we go! Can you tell this isn't a bona fide human being? I sure can't!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've brought the parrot.

lines.speaker: Eadgar
lines.text: Hand it here...there we go! Can you tell this isn't a bona fide human being? I sure can't!

lines.speaker: Fake man
lines.text: What am I doing here? Ow! Where's the rest of the Guard? Agh! I won't tell you anything!

sections.title: Talking to Eadgar after getting the fake man

lines.speaker: Eadgar
lines.text: Have you given the fake man to the troll cook yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Eadgar
lines.text: Go on then, give it to the cook!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No. I lost it.

lines.speaker: Eadgar
lines.text: You bumbling imbecile!

lines.speaker: Eadgar
lines.text: Never mind, here's one I prepared earlier.

lines.speaker: Eadgar
lines.text: Anything else I can do for you?

sections.title: Talking to Burntmeat while holding the fake man

lines.speaker: Burntmeat
lines.text: Did you find tasty human? Burntmeat smell something good.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes! Look!

lines.speaker: Fake man
lines.text: Heeeeeeelp!

lines.speaker: Burntmeat
lines.text: Ah, dat look like nice tasty human.

lines.speaker: Fake man
lines.text: Aaaargh! Somebody save me!

lines.speaker: Burntmeat
lines.text: Yep, sound like human too. Burntmeat put it in stew. Good work, human. Burntmeat give precious reward.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This is burnt meat.

lines.speaker: Burntmeat
lines.text: It first thing I ever try to cook! Very precious to Burntmeat.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you... and how's the stew?

lines.speaker: Burntmeat
lines.text: Slurp, mmm... Human stew cheer Burntmeat up!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, where can I get some goutweed?

lines.speaker: Burntmeat
lines.text: Hah! Trolls pick it all until none left, many years ago. Only remaining stock in storeroom.

lines.speaker: Burntmeat
lines.text: It well guarded, and Burntmeat hide key in fake bottom of kitchen drawer. Nobody find it there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's some well-guarded secret alright. I'll just be off on my way now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll be going now.

lines.speaker: Burntmeat
lines.text: Bye, bye.

sections.title: Talking to Burntmeat after giving him the fake man

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How's the stew?

lines.speaker: Burntmeat
lines.text: Slurp, mmm... Human stew cheer Burntmeat up!

sections.title: Getting the goutweed
Searching the storeroom drawers
Using the key on the storeroom door
Being caught by a goutweed guard troll
Searching the goutweed crate

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Hm?

sections.title: Talking to Eadgar after getting access to the storeroom

lines.speaker: Eadgar
lines.text: Well? Did the plan work?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes! I can get into the storeroom now.

lines.speaker: Eadgar
lines.text: Glad to hear it!

lines.speaker: Eadgar
lines.text: Anything else I can do for you?

sections.title: Talking to Sanfew after getting goutweed

lines.speaker: Sanfew
lines.text: What can I do for you young 'un?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have some goutweed!

lines.speaker: Sanfew
lines.text: Excellent! I will be able to complete the next part of the ritual now. I will teach you a new spell and give you some of my knowledge in Herblore as a token of thanks.

lines.speaker: Sanfew
lines.text: If you ever come across more goutweed, bring it to me; I don't need any more for the ritual, but it's still quite difficult for me to get. I'll exchange it for some other herbs.

sections.title: Post-quest
Talking to Eadgar after finishing the quest

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Eadgar
lines.text: Welcome to Mad Eadgar's! Serving all day, every day!

lines.speaker: Eadgar
lines.text: Would you care to sample our delicious home cooking?

sections.title: Talking to Sanfew after finishing the quest

lines.speaker: Sanfew
lines.text: What can I do for you young 'un?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have some more goutweed for you.

lines.speaker: Sanfew
lines.text: Ah, good. Here are some herbs in exchange.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have you any more work for me to help reclaim the stone circle?

lines.speaker: Sanfew
lines.text: Well, not right now I don't think young 'un. In fact, I need to make some more preparations myself for the ritual. Rest assured, if I need any more help I will ask you again.
